source_pr.title: infoschema: remove the ID and NAME columns of TIDB_CLUSTER_INFO
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: Lonng <heng@lonng.org>

<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

Fix issue https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/13586

### What is changed and how it works?

We should remove the `NAME` and `ID` columns of `TIDB_CLUSTER_INFO` system table because they are not the real stable value for cluster topology according to https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/ec2d6b7f536e8ecfa7cfd2dafe39d7585f7557fb/infoschema/tables.go#L1910.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Release note

 - No need.

symptoms: The test asserted exact columns/values for `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TIDB_CLUSTER_INFO/TIDB_CLUSTER_CONFIG`, but some metadata fields (like NAME/ID) are not stable for cluster topology.
failure_signature: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: The patch removes NAME/ID columns because they are not stable values for cluster topology; no concrete flaky failure signature is provided beyond deterministic test expectation updates.
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR changes the schema/columns of system tables and updates unit-test expectations accordingly (removing NAME/ID columns because they are not stable for cluster topology). The available case evidence does not provide a concrete intermittent failure signature or a targeted stabilization change that clearly maps to an existing root-cause category, so avoid hard-guessing and keep it as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select TYPE, `KEY`, VALUE from information_schema.tidb_cluster_config where `key`='key3.key4.nest4' order by type").Check(testkit.Rows( (infoschema/tables_test.go)
